mappings.rationale: Directly covers perception including motion perception, colour, visual system, and the translation of sensory stimulation into organized experience (C).
Covers vision including the retina, transduction, eye movements, colour vision, and cortical responses to visual stimuli (B.2).
Covers the cerebral cortex, sensory processing, and the neural mechanisms underlying attention, sensation, and perception (A.5, B.2.d).
Covers brain imaging, cognition, and neural basis of attention and perceptual processing (A.5, A.6).
Directly relevant to awareness of internal and external events and the problem of defining attention (A).
Covers acoustics, pitch, loudness, frequency, and the Doppler effect, directly relevant to audition mechanisms (B.3).
Covers optics and wave-particle duality of light, the physical basis of the visual stimulus and transduction process (B.2.b).
Directly covers the sensory experience of pain including cause, function, qualities, theories, and modes of treatment (B.4.d).
Covers olfactory receptors, olfactory processes, odour-inducing factors, and effects on behaviour (B.8).
Covers physiological mechanisms of the senses in general, relevant to sensory reception and transduction (B.1).
Covers the central and peripheral nervous systems relevant to sensory pathways, vestibular systems, and kinesthesis (B.5, B.6).
A perceptual phenomenon relevant to interrelations among the senses and individual differences in perceptual functioning (E.3, C.4).
Relevant to context effects in perception -- how previously experienced stimuli influence the observer (C.3.b).
Covers perception and reasoning, relevant to information-theory analysis of attentive phenomena (A.6).
Provides the broad psychological framework for understanding sensation, perception, and attention, including Gestalt and behaviourist theories (C.1).
Covers wave physics underlying both audition (sound waves) and vision (electromagnetic radiation), the physical basis of sensory stimulation.
Covers auditory perception and processing of complex sounds, relevant to audition mechanisms and perceptual organization of sound (B.3, C).
Covers hallucinations and altered perceptual states with neurological factors (G.2).
Relevant to differences in perceptual functioning among individuals and atypical attention and sensory processing (C.4, A.4).
Covers hallucinations including neurological factors and types of hallucinatory experience (G.2).
Emotions influence attention and perception; relevant to determinants of attention including temperament and unconscious influences (A.4).
Drugs are relevant to factors affecting perception and are linked to hallucinatory experiences (G.2) and altered perceptual states.
